resource_notes.change: all64 dispatches to one owner-CTA T.Kernel with grid=num_blocks_m*2 and 512 threads. Each M64 tile serially runs every gate/up pack2 N tile, synchronizes, then serially runs every down pack2 N tile. There is no grid synchronization or atomic operation.
resource_notes.small_shape: hidden=256, intermediate=256, experts=4, valid=568, seed=81394: compiled, allclose=true, max_abs_error=7.62939453125e-06, mean_ms=0.11400533715883891.
resource_notes.formal_protocol: p2 used seed=81394, warmup=10, iters=100, --check and passed allclose with max_abs_error=0.0009765625.
resource_notes.launches: One launch versus the baseline's separate gate/up and down launches.
resource_notes.shared_memory: The owner CTA allocates exactly two 128x128 float16 weight buffers, 65536 bytes total, reused as gate/up and then down0/down1. The 64x128 float16 activation fragment is also reused.
resource_notes.register_spill: Four 64x128 FP32 accumulator fragments are allocated for gate/up; down reuses the first two. A verified 64-element float16 routed-weight fragment is separate. The default TileLang/MACA compiler emitted no register-count or spill diagnostics.
resource_notes.swizzle: A single kernel can carry only one swizzle attribute. The owner kernel keeps the gate/up shape-selected swizzle (16 for p2); therefore the down phase cannot independently retain its former swizzle 8. This is an unavoidable structural consequence of the requested single launch.
resource_notes.mechanism: For p2 each owner CTA serializes 8 gate/up by tasks and 28 down by tasks. The loss of N-dimension CTA parallelism and the heavier long-lived fragment footprint dominate the saved launch boundary.
resource_notes.stopped_early: p2 regressed by 95.095796%, so p1, p3, and random-groups seeds were not run under the required early-stop rule.
